summary: Iteration 2 (push notifications + real PC hardware detection) fully verified. Backend: 29/29 pytest tests PASS including 12 NEW tests (push subscribe/unsubscribe/test/vapid-key, agent/token idempotency + auth, report-specs with valid/invalid X-Agent-Token, pc-specs read-back with persistence, desktop-agent download now 401 unauth / 200 authed with token+backend injected placeholders replaced, and manual-price-drop-now-creates-notification). Frontend: admin login → topbar bell dropdown shows toggle-push-btn (data-testid ok), /app/desktop shows download-agent-btn AND 'Il mio PC' spec fields (CPU=Ryzen 7 5800X, GPU=RTX 3070, RAM=32 GB, OS=Windows 11), /app/advisor shows specs-badge='Consigli personalizzati per: Ryzen 7 5800X · RTX 3070'. Advisor chat streams real Claude response and system prompt uses the stored specs (response referenced the Ryzen 7 5800X directly). Regression pass: auth, tracker, manual/target price, notifications, stats, builds all green. Web-push real delivery cannot be tested headlessly (fake endpoint) but /api/push/test returns 200 gracefully without crashing.

minor.issue: The injected BACKEND_URL comes from env FRONTEND_URL (default 'http://localhost:8001'). It works because FRONTEND_URL is set to the public preview URL in backend/.env, but semantically the agent should hit the BACKEND URL, not the frontend. Consider renaming env or adding BACKEND_URL env for clarity.
No schema validation on subscription.endpoint / keys — a client could submit an empty dict and it would still upsert. Consider Pydantic sub-model.

action_items: OPTIONAL: introduce a dedicated BACKEND_URL env for desktop-agent injection instead of reusing FRONTEND_URL.
OPTIONAL: strict Pydantic validation on PushSubInput.subscription (require endpoint + keys.p256dh + keys.auth).
OPTIONAL: purge dead push subscriptions on 401/403 (currently only 404/410 are cleaned).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:425 uses os.environ.get('FRONTEND_URL', 'http://localhost:8001') as the BACKEND_URL for the agent script — coincidentally works because same public host serves both, but the naming is misleading.
push.py silently swallows all non-404/410 WebPushException — good for uptime, but a repeated bad private key would go unnoticed; consider a metrics counter.
server.py: create_notification always calls push.send_push_to_user without try/except — push.py itself handles errors, but a future refactor could regress this. Consider wrapping defensively.
desktop_agent.py AGENT_SCRIPT is a large embedded raw string (~180 lines) — consider moving to a .py.tpl file for easier maintenance/linting.
server.py: agent_tokens have no rotation / revocation endpoint — a leaked token is valid forever. Minor for MVP.
test_credentials: Admin admin@boostpc.io/admin123 works and has stored PC specs (Ryzen 7 5800X / RTX 3070 / 32 GB / Windows 11).
seed_data_creation: Per-test users created via /api/auth/register with unique UUID suffix. Admin's specs persist across runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run backend tests serial: `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/backend_test.py -n 0`. All 29 tests should pass. Frontend routes: /login, /register, /app (dashboard), /app/tracker, /app/tracker/:id, /app/advisor, /app/builds, /app/desktop. Key testids added this iteration: toggle-push-btn (in Layout notifications dropdown, only visible when Notification API supported — always true in Chromium), download-agent-btn, spec-cpu/spec-gpu/spec-ram/spec-os (on Desktop page), specs-badge (on Advisor when user has specs). Push actual delivery cannot be validated headlessly — endpoints just need to not crash.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues. All new features working end-to-end. AI streaming personalization confirmed live (Claude response referenced the Ryzen 7 5800X from stored specs). Manual price drop now correctly triggers both in-app notification AND push send attempt (verified via test_lower_manual_price_creates_notification).
